svg.aria-label: § 7.3.1 Warp Collective Operation Hierarchy — 4-operation latency bars, redux.sync hardware reduction tree vs shfl_xor software butterfly tree, 3.77x speedup comparison

g.text: Warp Reduction: Hardware vs Software Paths
SM_120 SMSP · 4-operation latency hierarchy · redux.sync reduction tree vs shfl_xor butterfly tree · 3.77× speedup
A · WARP COLLECTIVE OPERATION LATENCY HIERARCHY
Four distinct hardware paths — latency spans 7.5× from fastest to slowest
Single uniform value, no per-lane variation
Zero crossbar traversals — dedicated tree handles everything
C · SOFTWARE PATH: shfl_xor 5-stage butterfly tree
→ Each lane has the FULL sum
Intermediate partial sums available — supports prefix scan
Best for: warp-wide sum/min/max → single scalar
Best for: prefix scan, intermediate partials needed
redux.sync writes to a UNIFORM register (UR) — a single scalar broadcast to all lanes. shfl produces per-lane R registers where each lane independently holds the full reduction result.
If intermediate partial sums are needed (e.g., prefix scan / exclusive scan), shfl is still required — redux.sync cannot expose intermediate values.
→ Warp-wide reduction to a single scalar (sum, min, max)
→ Result fits in a uniform register (UR) and broadcasts to all lanes
→ Prefix scan / exclusive scan needed (intermediate partials)
